mechanism_narrative: Month-to-month growth in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue expectations; when RSAFS momentum is positive, the mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value before 2011-10-15 was 395472
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum instances do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative momentum instances over rolling 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A renewed rise in unemployment above an already elevated level signals weakening income growth and recession risk; when UNRATE rises sharply, expected earnings fall and 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while UNRATE is above 8.0
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value before 2011-10-15 was 9.1
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or average triggered returns are not below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate pinned near zero lowers cash returns and discount rates, forcing portfolio reallocation into risky assets; when FEDFUNDS is at the zero lower bound, 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is less than or equal to 0.25 for at least 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value before 2011-10-15 was 0.08
failure_condition: Reject if zero-lower-bound episodes have lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-zero-lower-bound episodes in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Joint payroll expansion and falling unemployment confirm broad labor-market improvement, raising household income and earnings expectations; this interaction predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value before 2011-10-15 was 131334
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down state has no positive incremental effect over either single series in predicting 60-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with falling industrial production signals stagflationary margin pressure; the interaction reduces real demand and profit expectations, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and INDPRO 3-month change is negative
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value before 2011-10-15 was 226.268
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-up and INDPRO-down interaction does not produce lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with CPI-up alone or INDPRO-down alone.

mechanism_narrative: A downward break in industrial production signals weakening output and inventory adjustment, cutting near-term earnings expectations; when INDPRO crosses below its 6-month average, 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses from above to below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value before 2011-10-15 was 94.0419
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside crosses have nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or a hit rate below 52% for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts from depressed levels signals improving construction demand and household balance-sheet repair; when HOUST is low but rising, 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is below 700 and its 3-month change is greater than 5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value before 2011-10-15 was 571
failure_condition: Reject if low-and-rising HOUST episodes do not outperform low-and-falling HOUST episodes over subsequent 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer prices raises real-income pressure and increases the probability of tighter policy guidance; when CPIAUCSL inflation momentum crosses higher, 5-day S&P 500 returns are negative.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value before 2011-10-15 was 226.268
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration crosses above 3.0% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered cases.
